steps.title: When does a linear system have no solution?
steps.content: Two lines have no point in common when they are parallel: same slope but different $y$-intercepts. We need to find the choice whose two equations match that pattern.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $x=4$ is a vertical line and $y=7$ is a horizontal line; they cross at $(4,7)$. One solution.
  • B) $y=8x+4$ has slope $8$ and $y=3x+4$ has slope $3$; different slopes intersect once (here at $(0,4)$). One solution.
  • C) $y=12x+2$ and $y=12x+17$ have the same slope $12$ but different intercepts $2$ and $17$. Parallel, no solution.
  • D) $y=6$ has slope $0$ and $y=4x+6$ has slope $4$; different slopes intersect once (at $(0,6)$). One solution.
That is the correct answer.
